theory.intro: Triangles can be named by comparing the lengths of their sides. Equal sides are shown with matching tick marks.
facts: **Equilateral**: all three sides equal (all three angles equal too).
**Scalene**: all three sides different — no equal sides.
steps: Look at the tick marks (or given lengths) on each side.
Count how many sides are equal.
Three equal $\Rightarrow$ equilateral; two equal $\Rightarrow$ isosceles; none equal $\Rightarrow$ scalene.
